mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth signals expanding corporate output and earnings capacity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations when INDPRO is increasing over the prior 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, restrictive credit conditions elevate borrowing costs and suppress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rising unemployment and falling industrial production jointly signal recessionary contraction, where labor market deterioration amplifies supply-side weakness to produce negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2pp over 3 months while INDPRO declines over same period
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint UNRATE-INDPRO signal falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or negative return frequency is less than 55% during triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, declining retail sales signal consumer demand exhaustion that precedes earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of late-cycle instances when RSAFS is declining.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect construction sector momentum and household confidence that translates into broad demand spillovers, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive HOUST momentum fails to produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation combined with restrictive federal funds rates compresses real corporate margins and triggers valuation derating, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when both series are jointly elevated.
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with above-target inflation
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS are jointly elevated.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 0.3 percentage point increase in the unemployment rate over two months signals labor market deterioration that reduces household income expectations and consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp in 2 months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth signals aggregate demand expansion that lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 surprises are associated with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of quarterly observations.
